name: The Rise and Fall
description: A protagonist ascends to power or prominence and then declines due to flaw, corruption, or shifting circumstance.

nodes.label: Humble Origin
nodes.definition: The protagonist starts from a position of low status, limited power, or obscurity. They possess ambition, talent, or ruthlessness that the audience can identify early.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the character's starting position and driving ambition
exit_conditions: An opportunity or opening for advancement appears
typical_variants: Small-time criminal with big aspirations
Talented outsider denied access to power
Ambitious newcomer in a competitive field
Ordinary person who discovers an exploitable advantage
failure_modes: Character starts too high — no rise to dramatize
Ambition is not visible early, making the rise feel random
signals_in_text: Small acts that foreshadow the methods of the rise

nodes.label: First Opportunity
nodes.definition: A chance for advancement presents itself — a gap in the hierarchy, a lucky break, or a moment where the character's specific talent or ruthlessness is exactly what's needed.
exit_conditions: The protagonist seizes the opportunity, often through means that foreshadow the eventual fall
typical_variants: A power vacuum to exploit
A mentor or patron who opens doors
A criminal opportunity with outsized reward
Being in the right place with the right skill
failure_modes: Opportunity is too easy — no risk or cleverness required
Opportunity is unrelated to the character's qualities

nodes.label: Rapid Ascent
nodes.definition: The protagonist rises quickly — gaining power, wealth, status, or influence at an accelerating rate. Success breeds success, and the methods that work are reinforced.
entry_conditions: The first opportunity has been seized
The protagonist's methods are producing results
exit_conditions: The protagonist reaches a level of power that makes them a major figure
typical_variants: Building a criminal empire
Climbing the corporate or political ladder
Accumulating wealth through innovation or exploitation
Gaining public fame and adulation
failure_modes: Ascent is too slow and loses momentum
No moral compromises during the rise — the fall will lack causal connection

nodes.label: Peak of Power
nodes.definition: The protagonist reaches maximum power, wealth, or influence. They appear invulnerable. This is the pivot — everything after this is descent.
entry_conditions: The protagonist has achieved their visible goal
exit_conditions: The first serious crack appears — a challenge, an overreach, or a consequence that can't be managed
typical_variants: Controlling an empire
Public adulation at its height
Defeating all visible rivals
A celebration or display of power
failure_modes: Peak lasts too long — tension dissipates
No visible seeds of the coming fall are present at the peak
signals_in_text: The last moment of triumph before things turn

nodes.label: Cracks Form
nodes.definition: The methods that enabled the rise begin to generate consequences. Enemies multiply, allies become unreliable, the protagonist's flaw deepens, and the structure of power starts to erode.
entry_conditions: The first consequence of the rise's methods materializes
exit_conditions: The protagonist's position is clearly unsustainable; the fall is accelerating
typical_variants: Betrayal by a trusted lieutenant
Law enforcement or rivals closing in
The protagonist's paranoia driving away allies
Overreach that provokes unified opposition
failure_modes: Cracks are caused by bad luck rather than by the character's methods
The protagonist is never aware that things are slipping — no dramatic irony or self-knowledge
signals_in_text: The character alone in a big room

nodes.label: Desperate Grip
nodes.definition: The protagonist fights to maintain power through increasingly extreme measures. The very qualities that enabled the rise — ambition, ruthlessness, charisma — are now turned to destructive ends.
entry_conditions: The protagonist refuses to accept decline
exit_conditions: The protagonist's actions precipitate the final collapse
typical_variants: Violent crackdown on dissent
Burning bridges with remaining allies
A single catastrophic decision born of desperation
Attempting to destroy evidence or silence witnesses
failure_modes: Desperate measures succeed — the fall never comes
The protagonist gives up without fighting, reducing dramatic tension

nodes.label: The Fall
nodes.definition: The protagonist loses everything — power, freedom, relationships, or life. The fall is proportional to the height of the rise and causally connected to the methods used to achieve it.
exit_conditions: The protagonist's power is gone; their fate is sealed
typical_variants: Arrest and imprisonment
Violent overthrow or assassination
Public disgrace and exile
Abandoned by everyone, dying alone
failure_modes: Fall is caused by random misfortune rather than the character's own actions
Fall is so quick it has no weight
Fall is presented as unjust rather than consequential
signals_in_text: A final look at what was built

nodes.label: Legacy
nodes.definition: What remains after the fall — the impact on others, the lesson, the cautionary tale. The world goes on, shaped by what the protagonist built and destroyed.
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established in the protagonist's absence
typical_variants: Others inherit the wreckage
The system the protagonist exploited continues unchanged
A successor faces the same temptation
The story becomes a warning retold by others
failure_modes: Legacy is ignored — the story ends at the fall with no reflection
Legacy is too redemptive — the fall's consequences are softened
signals_in_text: A news report or court proceeding

edges.label: Seizes the Chance
edges.preconditions: Humble origin is established; an opening appears
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character enters a game with higher rewards and higher risks
edges.effects_on_character: Ambition is activated; the character commits to the pursuit of power
edges.common_alternatives: Opportunity is found by the character; opportunity finds the character
edges.anti_patterns: Opportunity requires no initiative or risk from the character

edges.label: Climbs Fast
edges.preconditions: The first opportunity is seized; the character's methods produce results
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each success raises the height from which the character will eventually fall
edges.effects_on_character: Methods are reinforced; moral compromises become routine
edges.common_alternatives: Ascent is through talent; ascent is through ruthlessness; ascent is through charm
edges.anti_patterns: Ascent involves no moral cost or compromise

edges.label: Reaches the Top
edges.preconditions: The ascent has proceeded; the character reaches their visible goal
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum height — everything achieved is now at risk
edges.effects_on_character: The character is committed to maintaining what they've built; alternatives are foreclosed
edges.common_alternatives: Peak is a single triumphant moment; peak is a sustained period of dominance
edges.anti_patterns: Peak has no sense of precariousness

edges.label: Erosion Begins
edges.preconditions: The peak is reached; the first consequence of the rise's methods appears
edges.effects_on_stakes: What seemed stable is shown to be fragile
edges.effects_on_character: Denial, paranoia, or doubling down as the character refuses to acknowledge decline
edges.common_alternatives: Cracks come from enemies; cracks come from allies; cracks come from the character's own excess
edges.anti_patterns: Erosion is caused purely by external bad luck

edges.label: Clings to Power
edges.preconditions: The cracks are visible; the character refuses to let go
edges.effects_on_stakes: Each desperate act makes the eventual fall more catastrophic
edges.effects_on_character: The worst version of the character emerges under pressure
edges.common_alternatives: Grip is violent; grip is strategic but increasingly reckless; grip is delusional
edges.anti_patterns: The character accepts decline gracefully — no desperate grip means a different archetype

edges.label: Everything Collapses
edges.preconditions: The desperate grip has failed; the final consequence arrives
edges.effects_on_stakes: Total loss — everything the character built is gone
edges.effects_on_character: The character faces the full consequences of their choices
edges.common_alternatives: Fall is sudden; fall is drawn out; the character sees it coming but can't stop it
edges.anti_patterns: Fall is caused by something unrelated to the character's rise or methods

edges.label: World Moves On
edges.preconditions: The fall is complete; the character's story is over
edges.effects_on_stakes: External stakes dissolve; the meaning of the rise and fall is crystallized
edges.effects_on_character: The character's legacy — cautionary, mourned, or forgotten — is established
edges.common_alternatives: World is better off; world is unchanged; world repeats the cycle
edges.anti_patterns: Legacy is too redemptive — the fall's weight is undercut
